term_or_item: (1) keeping the exact original text untouched
ordinary_reading: Do not silently mutate the raw source wording itself.
dangerous_alternative_reading: Forbid any external framing, metadata, or support context around the excerpt.
why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: If expanded too far, item (1) starts fighting legitimate non-mutating study support layers.
recommended_handling: Separate raw source fidelity from permissible external add-on context.

term_or_item: (2) making the excerpt understandable on its own
ordinary_reading: Enough local context or framing that the owner need not hunt manually.
dangerous_alternative_reading: Meaning-bearing text has been cut out or mutilated so comprehension is damaged.
why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: The ordinary reading is recoverable friction; the dangerous reading approaches corruption severity.
recommended_handling: Score these as two different subcases, not one item.

term_or_item: (3) making the excerpt the right size
why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: The owner is not talking about brevity preference but about preventing topic scrambling.
recommended_handling: Treat it as granularity fitness, not word-count minimization.

term_or_item: (4) making the excerpt useful to study from
ordinary_reading: Make the final teaching unit educationally powerful.
dangerous_alternative_reading: Any intervention is allowed if it feels more useful.
why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: That false reading would erase the integrity boundary and legitimize mutation.
recommended_handling: Preserve it as a top-level objective constrained by integrity.

ordinary_reading: Good to learn from in the context of the full teaching unit.
dangerous_alternative_reading: Necessarily complete without any auxiliary support layers.
why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: The owner architecture allows external context and metadata as long as the raw source remains untouched.
recommended_handling: Keep study-readiness compatible with layered support, not just raw isolation.

why_reading_shift_changes_the_answer: Confusing these would force a false binary between fidelity and teachability.
recommended_handling: Use 'untouched raw source text' for the source layer and allow clearly separated support layers.
